original_hypothesis.title: Re-whitelisting a Market Creates Reward Accounting Discontinuity
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When a market is re-whitelisted, lastRewardBlock is set to block.number, but accCantoPerShare is not reset. Users who maintained deposits during the de-whitelisted period will have their rewardDebt calculated against the old accumulator upon withdrawal, causing incorrect reward calculations.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: State transitions for administrative actions must maintain accounting invariants. The failure to properly adjust accumulators on re-whitelisting corrupts user balances.
preconditions: Governance de-whitelists and then re-whitelists a market
Users maintained deposits during the gap

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: lastRewardBlock is updated but accCantoPerShare is left stale
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Missing accumulator adjustment logic for re-whitelisting

analysis_questions: How exactly does the stale accumulator affect a user's rewardDebt upon their next sync_ledger call?
Can this lead to underflows in claim()?
validation_requirements: Trace the math for a user who deposits, waits, experiences a de-whitelist/re-whitelist cycle, and then claims

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Market is de-whitelisted and later re-whitelisted. A user who deposited before the de-whitelisting calls claim(). Their rewards are calculated using the stale accumulator, potentially granting them phantom rewards or causing an underflow depending on how the market supply changed.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Users who held deposits through a de-whitelist cycle
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Difficult to correct without manual state manipulation

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests for the full lifecycle of de-whitelisting and re-whitelisting

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
